title: Oldenlandia tetrangularis
intro: Oldenlandia tetrangularis, also known as Oldenlandia, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia tetrangularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Oldenlandia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to manage fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia tetrangularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Oldenlandia, which consists of several species of annual or perennial herbs. The species Oldenlandia tetrangularis is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as South Africa and Mozambique. The classification of Oldenlandia tetrangularis is further detailed at the subfamily level as Oldenlandioideae. The species is part of the larger order Lamiales in the plant kingdom.
uses: Oldenlandia tetrangularis, also known as the square-stemmed oldenlandia, has been found to have various medicinal properties in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves have been used as a treatment for eye infections and swelling. In some cultures, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for wound care and skin issues. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Oldenlandia tetrangularis for medicinal purposes.
